title: Validate shader compilation before scene activation

assertions: hasErrors() returns false on a fresh collector
getErrors() returns an empty array on a fresh collector

tests.description: collect() adds an error record and hasErrors() returns true
steps: Call collect() with a mock GL context, shader reference, shader type 'VERTEX', and error message 'ERROR: 0:5: undeclared identifier'
assertions: hasErrors() returns true after a single collect() call

tests.description: getErrors() returns all collected error records with correct fields
steps: Call collect() twice with different shader types and messages — once for VERTEX, once for FRAGMENT
Assert each record contains the shaderType, message, and a formatted summary string
assertions: getErrors() returns 2 records after 2 collect() calls
First record has shaderType 'VERTEX' and the corresponding error message
Second record has shaderType 'FRAGMENT' and the corresponding error message
Each record has a non-empty formatted summary string

tests.description: clear() resets the collector to empty state
assertions: hasErrors() returns false after clear()
getErrors() returns empty array after clear()

tests.description: Formatted summary includes shader type and source snippet
steps: Call collect() with shaderType 'VERTEX' and a message containing line info
Check the formatted summary string contains 'VERTEX' and a portion of the error message
assertions: Formatted summary contains the shader type string
Formatted summary contains the error message or a truncated excerpt

tests.description: validateShaderCompilation returns silently when no errors are present
steps: Create a mock renderer with compile as vi.fn()
Create a ShaderErrorCollector (real instance, no errors added)
assertions: validateShaderCompilation does not throw when errorCollector has no errors
renderer.compile was called with scene and camera

tests.description: validateShaderCompilation throws when shader errors are present
steps: Create a mock renderer with compile as vi.fn()
Create a ShaderErrorCollector and pre-populate it with an error via collect()
assertions: validateShaderCompilation throws an Error when errorCollector.hasErrors() is true
The thrown error message contains 'shader' (case-insensitive)

tests.description: validateShaderCompilation logs each error via console.error before throwing
steps: Create a mock renderer with compile as vi.fn()
Create a ShaderErrorCollector and pre-populate it with 2 errors
Assert console.error was called at least twice (once per error)
assertions: console.error is called once per collected error before the throw
console.error call arguments include the formatted summary from each error

tests.description: Thrown error message includes shader type information
steps: Create a mock renderer and a ShaderErrorCollector pre-populated with a VERTEX error
Call validateShaderCompilation and catch the error
assertions: Error message includes the shader type (e.g., 'VERTEX' or 'vertex')

tests.description: renderer.compile() is called to force synchronous shader compilation
steps: Create a mock renderer with compile as vi.fn()
Assert renderer.compile was called exactly once with (scene, camera)
assertions: renderer.compile is called exactly once
renderer.compile receives the scene and camera as arguments

tests.description: initAllForValidation calls init() on ALL geometry systems, not just active
steps: Create 3 mock GeometrySystem instances with init, draw, setOpacity, cleanup as vi.fn()
Create a ModeManager with all 3 as mode entries
Assert all 3 systems received exactly one init() call
assertions: All 3 mock systems have init called exactly once
Each init call receives the same seed and params

tests.description: initAllForValidation sets opacity to 0 on non-active systems
steps: Create 3 mock GeometrySystem instances with setOpacity as vi.fn()
Create a ModeManager and call initAllForValidation(scene, seed, params)
Assert setOpacity(0) was called on the two non-active systems
Assert setOpacity was NOT called with 0 on the active system (or was called with 1)
assertions: Non-active systems receive setOpacity(0)
Active system does not receive setOpacity(0)

tests.description: cleanupInactive calls cleanup() only on non-active systems
steps: Create 3 mock GeometrySystem instances with cleanup as vi.fn()
Create a ModeManager and call initAllForValidation(scene, seed, params)
Assert cleanup() was called on the two non-active systems
Assert cleanup() was NOT called on the active system
assertions: cleanup() is called on each non-active system
cleanup() is NOT called on the active system

tests.description: cleanupInactive does not throw when cleanup is undefined on a system
steps: Create 3 mock GeometrySystem instances, one WITHOUT cleanup defined
Create a ModeManager and call initAllForValidation(scene, seed, params)
assertions: cleanupInactive() does not throw when a system lacks cleanup()

tests.description: After initAllForValidation + cleanupInactive, normal draw() still works for the active system
steps: Create 3 mock GeometrySystem instances with all methods
Create a ModeManager and call initAllForValidation(scene, seed, params)
Assert the active system's draw() was called
assertions: Active system's draw() is called after initAllForValidation + cleanupInactive
No errors thrown during draw after cleanup of inactive systems

tests.description: validateShaderCompilation works end-to-end with a real ShaderErrorCollector (no errors path)
steps: Create a mock renderer where compile is a no-op
Create a real THREE.Scene and PerspectiveCamera
Assert no throw and renderer.compile was invoked
assertions: Full happy path completes without throwing
renderer.compile is invoked exactly once

tests.description: initAllForValidation sets initialized flag — subsequent draw() works without calling init() again
steps: Create a ModeManager and call initAllForValidation(scene, seed, params)
Clear all init mock call histories
Assert active system's draw() was called
Assert no additional init() calls were made
assertions: draw() works after initAllForValidation without requiring another init()
No extra init() calls happen on draw after initAllForValidation

tests.description: Multiple errors from different shaders are stored independently
steps: Call collect() 3 times with different shader types and messages
Assert each error has distinct shaderType or message values
assertions: getErrors() returns exactly 3 distinct records
Records are returned in collection order

testFiles.mockingStrategy: No Three.js mocking needed. Tests the collector class in isolation. Mock only the GL context parameter (plain object with getShaderInfoLog as vi.fn()).

Mock renderer as { compile: vi.fn() }. Use real ShaderErrorCollector instances, pre-populated with errors for failure tests. Use real THREE.Scene and THREE.PerspectiveCamera (no WebGL needed for these). Spy on console.error.

Follow existing pattern: createMockGeometrySystemWithOpacity() extended with cleanup. Tests added under a new describe('US-048: Shader validation — ModeManager integration') block. Uses createTestScene() helper already in the file.

unitTestable: ShaderErrorCollector: collect, hasErrors, getErrors, clear, formatted summary
validateShaderCompilation: throw-on-error, pass-on-clean, console.error logging, renderer.compile invocation
ModeManager.initAllForValidation: all systems init'd, non-active opacity set to 0
ModeManager.cleanupInactive: only non-active systems cleaned up, active preserved
requiresBrowserVerification: Real GLSL shaders compile without errors on a real GPU
renderer.debug.onShaderError fires correctly for broken shaders
renderer.compile() triggers onShaderError synchronously in Three.js 0.183
No WebGLProgram VALIDATE_STATUS errors during normal startup
All three geometry systems render stable first frames after validation
